name: Platform Positioning
summary: Understand how Ivanti Neurons connects service, endpoint, mobility, automation, and digital experience capabilities.
details: Explain platform value in business terms.
Connect product capabilities to operational outcomes.
Describe where automation and visibility reduce manual effort.

name: Business Objects
summary: Business objects represent the data model behind records, processes, and service operations in Ivanti ITSM.
details: Understand what an object stores and how it is extended.
Know when to create custom fields or custom objects.
Explain how design affects automation, reporting, and maintainability.

name: Relationships
summary: Relationships connect records such as requests, tasks, owners, configuration items, and dependent process data.
details: Explain parent-child and linked record behavior.
Describe how relationships improve reporting and navigation.
Mention how relationship design affects automation and data quality.

name: Forms
summary: Forms define what users see and edit when they open records or submit service data.
details: Discuss field arrangement and role-based visibility.
Explain how forms support clean user experience.
Tie form design to request and fulfillment quality.

name: Layouts
summary: Layouts shape how record data is organized visually for different user needs and process stages.
details: Use layouts to reduce clutter.
Explain conditional visibility and user-specific views.
Describe why layout design matters for adoption.

name: Validations
summary: Validations protect data quality by enforcing required logic and preventing incorrect updates.
details: Use validations to stop incomplete or invalid submissions.
Explain the difference between UI behavior and business validation.
Mention maintainability and controlled logic placement.

name: Quick Actions
summary: Quick actions support repeatable record-level or saved-search-driven automation tasks.
details: Use quick actions for targeted repeatable operations.
Explain when they are better than broader workflow design.
Mention scheduled and context-aware usage.

name: Workflows
summary: Workflows model approvals, status movement, branching logic, tasks, and process automation.
details: Describe approvals, branching, and transitions.
Explain how workflows support requests and change processes.
Mention testing, exception handling, and governance.

name: Saved Searches
summary: Saved searches are reusable filtered datasets for operational views, reporting, dashboards, and automation.
details: Explain filtering and reusability.
Describe how saved searches support quick actions and reports.
Mention weekly reporting and communication scenarios.

name: Email Templates
summary: Templates standardize email content for notifications, escalations, and scheduled communications.
details: Mention consistency and reduced manual effort.
Explain dynamic fields and recipient logic.
Tie email templates to workflows and quick actions.

name: Escalations
summary: Escalations enforce response timing and drive attention when records exceed operational thresholds.
details: Explain schedule-driven and status-driven handling.
Connect escalations to SLAs and ownership.
Discuss testing and notification design.

name: Reporting and Dashboards
summary: Dashboards and reports provide visibility into operational trends, backlog, performance, and exceptions.
details: Connect reports to saved searches and KPIs.
Explain audience-specific dashboards.
Discuss trend visibility and decision support.

name: Roles and Permissions
summary: Roles and permissions control access to records, actions, data, and administrative capabilities.
details: Explain least-privilege thinking.
Discuss admin versus end-user access patterns.
Mention governance, testing, and operational support.

name: REST APIs
summary: REST API design covers methods, endpoints, authentication, payload structure, and operational reliability.
details: Explain request/response flow clearly.
Mention authentication, validation, and mapping.
Discuss logging, retries, and monitoring.

name: Authentication Patterns
summary: Authentication secures connections between Ivanti and external platforms.
details: Discuss API keys, tokens, or other secured methods at a high level.
Explain why credential handling and least privilege matter.
Mention secure storage and connection governance.

name: Web Service Patterns
summary: Web service patterns help move or synchronize data between Ivanti and external systems.
details: Describe payload transformation and response handling.
Explain scheduling versus event-driven invocation.
Discuss monitoring and exception paths.

name: Error Handling and Retries
summary: Reliable automation and integrations require retries, error capture, notifications, and safe rollback thinking.
details: Explain retry strategy and non-duplication.
Discuss logging and alerting.
Mention how to reduce silent failures.

name: Scripted Logic and Validation Design
summary: Scripted logic helps implement advanced validations, field behavior, and integration-specific control where configuration alone is not enough.
details: Explain when scripting is appropriate.
Mention maintainability and safe scope.
Avoid over-engineering when configuration can solve the need.

name: Policy Governance
summary: Policies drive operational and security behavior across managed devices and service processes.
details: Explain governance goals and exception handling.
Describe how policy design affects user experience.
Discuss enforcement, visibility, and remediation.

name: Device Enrollment
summary: Enrollment brings devices under management and establishes ownership, policy, and baseline controls.
details: Differentiate corporate-owned and BYOD onboarding.
Explain post-enrollment baseline actions.
Discuss user communication and compliance setup.

name: Ownership Models
summary: Ownership models shape what controls are applied, what data is visible, and how apps and compliance are managed.
details: Differentiate BYOD and corporate-owned expectations.
Explain governance and privacy considerations.
Tie ownership to compliance and app distribution.

name: Compliance Rules
summary: Compliance rules define what makes a device compliant and what happens when it is not.
details: Describe rule definition and thresholds.
Explain remediation and restricted access actions.
Connect policy violations to operational workflows.

name: App Deployment
summary: App deployment manages how required and approved applications reach managed devices.
details: Explain rollout planning and audience targeting.
Discuss versioning, compliance impact, and support.
Mention validation and failure handling.

name: Patch Rings and Rollout Waves
summary: Patch rings stage deployment through pilot and broader production waves to reduce risk.
details: Explain pilot groups and rollout criteria.
Discuss reporting and exception handling.
Connect phased rollout to enterprise reliability.

name: Distribution Jobs
summary: Distribution jobs push software or content to targeted devices using planned scheduling and validation.
details: Discuss package targeting and timing.
Explain outcome tracking and missed-device handling.
Connect distribution jobs to operational control.

name: Inventory and Discovery
summary: Inventory and discovery provide visibility into devices, attributes, and estate composition.
details: Explain why source quality matters.
Describe reconciliation between discovered and managed data.
Connect discovery to reporting and asset management.

name: Asset Lifecycle
summary: Asset lifecycle tracks planning, deployment, ownership, refresh, and retirement of technology assets.
details: Explain lifecycle states and accountability.
Connect lifecycle to cost and compliance visibility.
Discuss reconciliation with inventory sources.

name: Data Normalization and Reconciliation
summary: Normalization improves data consistency across discovered, managed, and manually maintained sources.
details: Explain duplicate handling and naming consistency.
Discuss source-of-truth considerations.
Connect reconciliation to reporting trustworthiness.

name: Velocity Browser Sessions
summary: Velocity helps transform browser-based enterprise workflows into more efficient frontline experiences.
details: Discuss simplification for handheld or kiosk use.
Explain how screen flow and prompt design matter.
Connect browser session improvements to operator speed.

name: Scanning Workflows
summary: Scanning workflows streamline repetitive frontline tasks and reduce manual input friction.
details: Explain scan-first interaction design.
Discuss error reduction and speed.
Connect workflow design to real operational outcomes.

name: Experience Monitoring
summary: Experience monitoring helps teams understand friction, visibility gaps, and user-impact trends.
details: Explain why visibility into user experience matters.
Connect monitoring to proactive improvement.
Discuss how insights can influence automation or support.

name: Workflow Design
summary: Workflow design connects operational steps into repeatable, governed processes.
details: Discuss approvals, branching, notifications, and exception paths.
Explain how workflows support different modules.
Mention how to design for clarity and maintainability.

name: Data Mapping
summary: Data mapping transforms incoming or outgoing payloads into the right format for Ivanti or connected systems.
details: Explain field alignment and transformation logic.
Discuss null handling, defaults, and identifiers.
Mention safe synchronization and idempotence.
